items.headline: Multiple garage conversions to JADUs issued in single week
items.summary: Four permits issued this week convert garages into junior accessory dwelling units (JADUs) across different neighborhoods. This burst of JADU permits suggests residents are responding to the state's streamlined approval process for these smaller units.

items.headline: Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan adopted
items.summary: Mountain View adopted a new Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan at the May 26 council meeting. The plan sets direction for park and recreation services in the city.

items.headline: City decarbonization five-year plan to be studied
items.summary: Council held a study session on May 26 to discuss Mountain View's decarbonization goal and a proposed five-year plan with specific actions to reduce emissions.

items.headline: City approves $1.6M agreement with Highway Patrol for FIFA World Cup traffic
items.summary: Santa Clara City Council and Stadium Authority jointly approved a Standard Agreement with the California Highway Patrol for traffic management services during the FIFA World Cup, with a projected cost of $1.6 million and related budget amendment.

items.headline: Equinix data center permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center building received a permit at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard. Data centers attract high-wage tech jobs and generate significant property tax revenue for the city.

items.headline: City agrees to Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo, to be installed at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument memorializes peace and survivors.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District sets FY 2026-27 budget
items.summary: The city approved the 2026-2027 budget and annual assessment for the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District, which funds improvements and services in that area.

items.headline: General Plan Health and Safety Element update scheduled
items.summary: Cupertino City Council is updating the Health and Safety Element of its General Plan (2015-2040). This is a foundational planning document that shapes how the city addresses public health, emergency preparedness, and safety infrastructure over the next 15 years.
